title: Ensure Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Project  Ownership Assignments/Changes

input_row.title: Ensure Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Project  Ownership Assignments/Changes
input_row.description: In order to prevent unnecessary project ownership assignments to users/service- accounts and further misuses of projects and resources, all roles/Owner assignments should be monitored. Members (users/Service-Accounts) with a role assignment to primitive role roles/Owner are project owners. The project owner has all the privileges on the project the role belongs to. These are summarized below: • All viewer permissions on all GCP Services within the project • Permissions for actions that modify the state of all GCP services within the project • Manage roles and permissions for a project and all resources within the project • Set up billing for a project Granting the owner role to a member (user/Service-Account) will allow that member to modify the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy. Therefore, grant the owner role only if the member has a legitimate purpose to manage the IAM policy. This is because the project IAM policy contains sensitive access control data. Having a minimal set of users allowed to manage IAM policy will simplify any auditing that may be necessary.
input_row.rationale: Project ownership has the highest level of privileges on a project. To avoid misuse of project resources, the project ownership assignment/change actions mentioned above should be monitored and alerted to concerned recipients. • Sending project ownership invites • Acceptance/Rejection of project ownership invite by user • Adding role\Owner to a user/service-account • Removing a user/Service account from role\Owner
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Log-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric is present with filter text: (protoPayload.serviceName="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com") AND (ProjectOwnership OR projectOwnerInvitee) OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="REMOVE" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="ADD" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") Ensure that the prescribed Alerting Policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of zero(0) for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for your organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with filter set to: (protoPayload.serviceName="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com") AND (ProjectOwnership OR projectOwnerInvitee) OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="REMOVE" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="ADD" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: (protoPayload.serviceName="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com") AND (ProjectOwnership OR projectOwnerInvitee) OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="REMOVE" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") OR (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="ADD" AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") 4. Click Submit Filter. The logs display based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and the Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the display prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the desired metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page opens. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create a prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud beta logging metrics create • Reference for Command Usage: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/metrics/create Create prescribed Alert Policy • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create • Reference for Command Usage: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/alpha/monitoring/policies/create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.
input_row.additional_information: 1. Project ownership assignments for a user cannot be done using the gcloud utility as assigning project ownership to a user requires sending, and the user accepting, an invitation. 2. Project Ownership assignment to a service account does not send any invites. SetIAMPolicy to role/owneris directly performed on service accounts.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be fully verified via GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud).
- The audit provides CLI commands that validate the presence of log metrics and alert policies.
- It is about IAM role management and logging, which can be queried via GCP APIs.
- The requirements can be efficiently verified against configuration settings using available tools.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_log_metric_project_ownership_assignments_monitored

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Log Metrics Check:**
   - Use the command `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to retrieve the existing log metrics.
   - The automated check should filter the output to see if there exists at least one log metric with the following filter:
     ```
     (protoPayload.serviceName="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com") 
     AND 
     (ProjectOwnership OR projectOwnerInvitee) 
     OR 
     (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="REMOVE" 
     AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner") 
     OR 
     (protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="ADD" 
     AND protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.role="roles/owner")
     ```

2. **Alerting Policy Check:**
   - Use the command `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` to retrieve the existing alerting policies.
   - Validate the output to ensure that at least one alert policy exists with conditions where:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"` 
     - `enabled` is set to `true`.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - For Log Metrics: The automated check passes if the specified log metric exists in the output.
   - For Alert Policies: The check passes if at least one alert policy matching the criteria is found.

This automated approach efficiently verifies compliance with the specified control while ensuring that the necessary logging and monitoring are effectively set up to manage project ownership assignments and changes.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is mostly correct, as the tasks described can be performed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). However, the complexity of interpreting the results and ensuring the correct configuration might require some manual verification steps to ensure completeness and accuracy.
- **GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/CLI Feasibility:** The tasks described can indeed be checked via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is appropriate for retrieving log metrics and alert policies, respectively.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly correct, but there are some areas that need clarification or improvement:
  - The filter for log metrics is not entirely clear. The logical grouping and syntax should be verified to ensure it captures all necessary conditions accurately.
  - The use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is correct, but the filter condition for alert policies should be more specific to ensure it captures the intended metrics and conditions.
- **Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Methods/CLI Commands:** The commands used are valid, but the `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` command is in the alpha stage, which may not be ideal for production environments due to potential instability or changes.
- **Program Name:** The program name `gcp_logging_log_metric_project_ownership_assignments_monitored` is descriptive but could be more concise. Consider including specific terms like 'role_change' or 'owner_assignment' for clarity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Filter Logic:** Ensure the filter logic for log metrics is correctly structured to avoid missing any critical events. Consider using parentheses to group conditions logically.
- **Alert Policy Details:** Specify the exact metric type and conditions expected in the alert policy to avoid ambiguity.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming to `gcp_logging_role_change_monitoring` for clarity and brevity.
- **Alternative Methods:** Explore using Google Cloud Monitoring API directly for more robust and potentially stable solutions compared to alpha commands.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- **Reasoning:** While the approach is generally sound, the reliance on alpha commands and potential complexity in filter logic reduce confidence slightly. Further validation and testing would be beneficial to ensure robustness.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Explanation:** While the automated approach is largely correct, improvements in filter logic clarity, command stability, and program naming are recommended. Consider refining the filter conditions and possibly using more stable API methods for alert policy checks.
